mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances where unemployment rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production indicate contracting manufacturing output, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not correlate with negative 20-day forward returns during tightening periods, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and wage expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive quarterly returns, with a walk-forward Rank IC consistently below 0.03 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDP growth and retail sales shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rebound in industrial production following a prolonged contraction indicates supply-side stabilization, reducing recession fears and triggering positive S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: Industrial production rebounds do not precede positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 45%.
